programme.title: Lionesses: How Football Came Home
programme.desc: The inside story of the England team's triumph at the UEFA Women's Euro 2022, featuring interviews with players as well as previously unseen footage and official highlights.

programme.title: QPR: The Four Year Plan
programme.desc: An insight into the running of Queens Park Rangers football club, as cameras were given unprecedented access to the boardroom and the first team. In 2007, the club was facing relegation from the Championship as well as financial ruin, before being bought by F1 supremo Bernie Ecclestone and Italian businessman Flavio Briatore. Soon after, they were joined on the board by Indian steel magnate Lakshm

programme.title: Stasi FC
programme.desc: Tracing a decade of football, this documentary tells the story of the East German secret service's control of BFC Dynamo, a tale of betrayal, murder and manipulation.

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before.

programme.title: Discovering: Clint Eastwood
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor who rose to fame in Sergio Leone's Spaghetti Westerns and won Oscars for directing Unforgiven and Million Dollar Baby.

programme.title: Discovering: Michael Caine
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who starred in Alfie and The Italian Job and won Oscars for his roles in Hannah and Her Sisters and The Cider House Rules.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of writer and film-maker Nora Ephron, who was nominated for Academy Awards for her screenplays for Silkwood, When Harry Met Sally and Sleepless in Seattle.

programme.title: Jackie Stewart
programme.desc: Documentary about Formula 1 great Jackie Stewart, from his humble beginnings outside Glasgow to winning three World Drivers' Championships.

programme.title: Man on Wire
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge.

programme.title: Schmeichel
programme.desc: Documentary profile of Peter Schmeichel, the goalkeeper who won the Euros with Denmark in 1992 and was part of Manchester United's 1999 treble-winning squad.

programme.title: Val
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the daily life of actor Val Kilmer, featuring never-before-seen footage spanning 40 years.

programme.title: Williams & Mansell: Red 5
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise of Nigel Mansell, the Williams F1 racing team and how they came together to fight for the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship.

programme.title: My Icon: Leon Pryce
programme.desc: The former Bradford Bulls and St Helens star discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and sporting career.

programme.title: Bobby Robson: More Than a Manager
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the career of the much-loved former England, Barcelona and Newcastle United manager. Via the Hand of God and Paul Gascoigne's tears, to titles across Europe, Robson overcame the most extreme challenges - including fighting for his life after being diagnosed with cancer - before returning to football to manage his beloved Newcastle.

programme.title: Gascoigne
programme.desc: Documentary about Paul Gascoigne, which charts his rise to fame from his humble roots in the North East to the world stage and his subsequent personal problems. The film features an extensive interview with the troubled former footballing superstar, as well as comment from those who admired his skills, including Wayne Rooney, Jose Mourinho and Gazza's ex-England and Tottenham Hotspur team-mate Gar

programme.title: Premier League 360 - Big Sam's Bolton
programme.desc: A look at Sam Allardyce's iconic Bolton team of the early 2000s in the Premier League.

programme.title: Everton: Howard's Way
programme.desc: Documentary film telling the story of Everton and how they reached their their trophy-winning peak in the 1980s under the stewardship of Howard Kendall.

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: Julz runs into a lion-hyena showdown and Henry goes on a house call to the Kakumbi den, where a pup has gone AWOL.

programme.title: Jaguar Journals
programme.desc: Biologist and conservationist Lizzie Daly uses the latest technology to follow a group of jaguars in the Pantanal region of South America to gain insight into their daily lives. Lizzie and the Oncafari team hunt for a missing dominant jaguar and monitor the battle for power between young hopefuls.

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Leading shark ecologist Mauricio Hoyos attempts a TV first as he attaches a camera to the dorsal fin of a great white shark off Guadalupe Island.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
programme.desc: A look at the stark karst plateau of caves and dry peaks in Austria's Totes Gebirge, hiding rich ecosystem where wildlife thrives against the odds.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Using CGI, archive footage and science, to witness how creatures from deep seas to land predators hunt, survive and dominate nature.

programme.title: Becoming Elephant: The Orphans of Reteti
programme.desc: Following the journey of orphaned elephants at Reteti Elephant Sanctuary, the first community-owned and operated elephant sanctuary in Africa.

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: An exploration of the remote wonder of the Exuma Cays, the Bahamas' first ocean park - a largely unexplored region made up of hundreds of tiny islands.

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part one of two. The reasons why young animals enjoy playing, and how it can teach them to survive in the wild. The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part two of two. The secrets behind animals' group games, from dolphins that play catch to a hyena tug of war, revealing the benefits they gain from their playful behaviour. The programme examines how breaching the rules of a game can have fatal consequences - but how it also presents an opportunity for animals with a rigid hierarchy to break down social barriers.

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: A global investigation into insect declines, exploring their diversity and their critical roles on the planet. Scientists reveal the beauty of bugs, from pollinators to insect assassins to the tiny clean-up crews that purify the planet.

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: Learning how bees, butterflies, moths and other pollinators keep the fields fruitful and exploring what life would be like if these busy bugs vanished.

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: Following a female king cobra in the hot forest of Southern India as she sets out to find a mate, find food and lay eggs before the monsoon arrives.

programme.title: Into the Wild: India
programme.desc: As the land dries up, matriarch elephant Anura races to get her herd to a distant watering hole. But her tiny grandson is struggling to keep up.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the challenges and freedom of animals that use the air to survive and thrive, with the opening edition demonstrating how different species use air, from rookies to the masters of the skies.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A look at the animals which take to the skies and therefore gain an advantage over their rivals in the eat-or-be-eaten game.

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee reveals the stories behind the origins of each of the world's primate species, coming face-to-face with some of the planet's most charismatic and mischievous monkeys. He begins his journey in Asia.

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee takes a closer look at the rise of the monkey, exploring the many diverse species and their place in primate evolution.

programme.title: Monkeys: An Amazing Animal Family
programme.desc: Biologist Patrick Aryee examines the development of monkeys from Central and South America and their evolutionary journey dating back more than 40 million years.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: From melodic songs to strange hums, David Attenborough shares the sounds that gibbons, lyrebirds, bull elk and more use to find a mate - and warn off the competition.

programme.desc: A look at how baby animals communicate in their quest for survival, using sound to beg and call for help in the early days of life.

programme.title: Matsalu Moose: Wild Giants of the Baltics
programme.desc: Matsalu National Park at the Baltic Sea is known for being one of Europe's most important stopover places for migrating birds. However, few know that an exceptionally large moose colony also lives here. This documentary follows the moose through the eyes of two yearlings as they are chased away from their mother before she gives birth to the next calf.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A family from the Dallas, Texas area aims to escape the summer city heat by scoring a bargain property on Cedar Creek Lake; a little bit of elbow grease goes a long way when the entire family pitches in to transform their new lake house into the perfect waterfront retreat. And with their dogs joining them, they add a few personal touches to make their four-legged friends feel right at home.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A Washington couple searches the region's lakes for a waterfront retreat close to the Pacific Ocean in Port Orchard; They hope to renovate a cozy cabin into a dreamy escape for their young family.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: After one trip to Lake Tillery, North Carolina, a couple decides to buy a lakefront vacation home there as an easy escape from their busy city lifestyle; they hope to put their own touch on a home where they can entertain family and friends.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: An Upstate New York couple dreams of purchasing a home along the shores of Oneida Lake; however, finding an affordable property that overlooks the water and has plenty of space for their family may require some sweat equity and an ambitious plan.

programme.title: Lakefront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: The tables are turned when two real estate agents decide to buy and renovate their own spot on the water after years selling lakefront properties.

programme.desc: A family looks for a vacation home on Logan Martin Lake, Alabama, in hopes of getaways filled with fishing, kayaking and relaxation.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The crew saves barn wood from a dangerous barn in Lewistown, PA.; Mark Bowe scouts an old log cabin in Missouri, a barn in New Hampshire and a home in Texas; Alex and Tim visit the longest covered bridge in Pennsylvania.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders restore a cabin with major challenges; Mark visits the finished log skin mountain home designed by Karen Tillery; Katherine Shelton works at the boneyard blacksmith shop and Alex makes a table from salvaged floorboards.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys restore a log cabin that has been in the Brown family since 1856, using wood from the original cabin as well as old logs from a nearby barn and new logs from a local saw mill.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A man and his fiancé would like to see their rustic camping cabin without plumbing transformed into a place they can entertain, use the bathroom indoors and enjoy multiple seasons; they'd also like to pay homage to their funeral home business.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team jumps at the opportunity to buy a friend's 100-year-old farmhouse in Manchester, Maine, and transform it into their headquarters; they plan to create a showroom, offices and a special space for designing and creating custom pieces.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: An early 1900s cabin that's intimately tied to Maine's history and legacy puts the team to the test as they try to re-create its original look and feel.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple fell in love with the views of Greenville Junction, Maine, but the cabin they purchased is in need of some serious work; Chase and the team create a working bathroom and kitchen as well as additional sleeping space.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A man who recently inherited his dad's winter retreat wants to honor his father's memory while making the camp comfortable for year-round living for his family with a bigger bathroom, more inside light, a four-season porch, and space for their dogs.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Cabin Masters help Chase's wife, Sarah, restore her late grandmother's cabin to preserve her memory; with only 12 weeks to build, the team works fast to update the interior with a new kitchen, bigger windows, and Chase's decorating style.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: A walk through the castle reveals decades of damage and neglect that will be a restoration challenge for Chip and Jo, but the landmark's rich history inspires their determination to preserve the original architecture.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: Jo develops her future vision for the castle with her styling team and commissions custom artisan pieces; Chip discovers new challenges regarding what can be salvaged and restored to original glory, and what is too far gone.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: Jo develops new paint colors inspired by the castle's one-hundred-year history, and she and Chip make big decisions about interior flooring and tile, as well as the exterior stone walls, that dramatically advance renovations.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: Unexpected water damage is a costly setback for Chip and Jo, but the installation of beautifully restored wood pieces and reclaimed tile from Europe start to restore the original grandeur of the castle.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: In the home stretch of construction, Chip and Jo focus on perfecting the details of the castle; a design issue in the kitchen spurs Chip to insist on a last-minute change to fix countertops and windows; Jo gets a surprise at the Silos.

programme.title: Fixer Upper: The Castle
programme.desc: Chip and Jo finish a fully restored three-story castle originally built in the early 1900s; they reveal the final design and share what it took to blend a vintage sensibility with modern comfort.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Chip and Jo meet with a client who just purchased a 1950s bungalow hoping to make it her forever home after losing her husband to cancer.
